title: At the Café
learningGoal: The learner will orally orchestrate a socially appropriate commercial transaction utilizing greeting protocols, modal softening ('Could you'), and polite closures ('Thank you', 'Have a good day').
instructions: You are a customer in a café. The worker is speaking to you. Remember what you learned about English culture! You must NOT give her orders. Reply ALOUD to her warmly, asking questions instead of giving commands.

contentItems.text: Smile when you speak! A happy voice is a polite voice. Remember to use 'Could you' and 'Please'.

speakingPrompt: Respond to the Hindi prompts aloud.
scenario: Ordering food gracefully in a café.

subTasks.prompt: वर्कर: 'Hello, how are you today?' (नमस्ते, आज आप कैसे हैं?) जवाब दें: 'मैं ठीक हूँ, धन्यवाद।' (I am fine...)
वर्कर: 'What can I get for you?' (मैं आपके लिए क्या ला सकती हूँ?) जवाब दें: 'क्या आप मुझे चाय दे सकती हैं, कृपया?' (Could you...)

sampleResponse.targetText: Could you give me tea, please?
sampleResponse.transliteration: कुड यू गिव मी टी, प्लीज़?
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: क्या आप मुझे चाय दे सकती हैं, कृपया?

subTasks.prompt: वर्कर: 'I'm sorry, we don't have tea right now. Would you like coffee?' (मुझे खेद है, अभी हमारे पास चाय नहीं है। क्या आप कॉफी लेना चाहेंगे?) जवाब दें: 'हाँ, कृपया।' (Yes...)
वर्कर: 'Here is your coffee. That is 5 dollars.' (यह रही आपकी कॉफी। 5 डॉलर हुए।) जवाब दें: 'धन्यवाद। क्या आप मुझे पानी दे सकती हैं?' (Thank you. Could you...)

sampleResponse.targetText: Thank you. Could you give me water?
sampleResponse.transliteration: थैंक यू। कुड यू गिव मी वाटर?
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: धन्यवाद। क्या आप मुझे पानी दे सकती हैं?

subTasks.prompt: वर्कर: 'Yes, of course. Have a good day!' (हाँ, बिलकुल। आपका दिन शुभ हो!) जवाब दें: 'आपका भी!' (You...)

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: Could you give me tea, please?
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: कुड यू गिव मी टी, प्लीज़?
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: क्या आप मुझे चाय दे सकती हैं, कृपया?

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: Thank you. Could you give me water?
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: थैंक यू। कुड यू गिव मी वाटर?
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: धन्यवाद। क्या आप मुझे पानी दे सकती हैं?

acceptableParaphrases: You have a good day too

scoringRubric.Command Avoidance: Did the user completely avoid saying 'Give me tea'?
scoringRubric.Response Protocols: Did the user say 'Yes, please' instead of a blunt 'Yes'?
scoringRubric.Prosody: Did the user deploy terminal rising pitch contours when asking the 'could you' questions?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: I am fine, thank you. Could you give me tea, please? Yes, please. Thank you. Could you give me water? You too!

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You sounded like a native speaker. You were warm, polite, and you avoided ordering the worker around by successfully using 'Could you'!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! A common mistake is saying 'Give me tea'. In English, you cannot directly translate Hindi commands. You must always use the modal wrapper: 'Could you give me tea'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you add 'Please' and 'Thank you' to your answers. Saying just 'Yes' to a waiter sounds aggressive.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Worker: Hello, how are you today?... (full dialogue)

hintText: Smile when you say 'Could you give me tea, please?⤴'. Make sure your voice goes UP at the end!
commonMistakes: Falling into L1 translation and saying 'Give me water'.
